content.full_text:
March 16, 2006

Iris P. Yang
McDonough Holland & Allen, PC
555 Capitol Mall, 9th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814-4692

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-026

Dear Ms. Yang:

This letter is in response to your request, on behalf of Yuba City Councilmember David Doolittle, for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person’s authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice from you or private counsel.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
BGL:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-026
